skill.name: hgnc-api
skill.description: Access the HGNC (HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee) database to search for and retrieve gene information including symbols, names, IDs, and other metadata.
skill.summary: Query the official HGNC database to retrieve standardized human gene nomenclature, symbols, and metadata.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 38 static analysis findings are false positives. The 9 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match argparse constructor calls, JSON score data, and markdown front matter, not actual cryptographic operations. The 12 network-related findings are legitimate HTTP requests to the official HGNC REST API (rest.genenames.org), which is the core function of this skill. The 17 external command findings in SKILL.md are markdown code block examples showing documented usage syntax, not actual command execution. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential theft, or business logic abuse was identified. The skill is a standard bioinformatics REST API client with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: User input embedded in URL path without sanitization
low_findings.description: The fetch() and search() functions embed user-supplied 'term' and 'field' values directly into URL path segments via f-strings. While the base URL is hardcoded to the legitimate HGNC API and no credentials are involved, path traversal characters in input could alter the API endpoint accessed.

content.user_title: Search and Retrieve Gene Data from HGNC
content.value_statement: Researchers waste time searching multiple sources for gene nomenclature data. This skill provides direct access to the HGNC database to retrieve standardized gene symbols, names, and identifiers through a single interface.
actual_capabilities: Fetch detailed gene records by symbol, ID, or custom field from the HGNC database
Search for genes using keywords with optional field-specific filters
Retrieve HGNC service status and metadata information
Output structured JSON results suitable for downstream analysis
Support reproducible gene nomenclature lookups in research workflows
limitations: Requires internet access to reach the HGNC REST API
Handles one query term at a time, no built-in batch processing
Output is limited to HGNC data only, not other gene databases
No local caching of previously fetched results

use_cases.title: Validate gene symbols for a research paper
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a manuscript verifies gene symbols used in their paper against the official HGNC database to ensure accuracy before submission.

use_cases.title: Build a gene reference table for bioinformatics analysis
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician retrieves official gene symbols and IDs to create a standardized lookup table for pipeline processing.

use_cases.title: Retrieve gene metadata for clinical reporting
use_cases.description: A medical writer looks up official gene names and identifiers to ensure accuracy in clinical documentation and reports.

prompt_templates.title: Look up a gene symbol
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the gene record for BRAF from the HGNC database and show the official symbol, name, and HGNC ID.

prompt_templates.title: Search genes by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the HGNC database for genes related to 'kinase' and show the matching symbols, IDs, and scores.

prompt_templates.title: Query by alternative identifier
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch gene records from HGNC using the 'tag' field to find all genes tagged with 'approved'.

prompt_templates.title: Validate multiple genes from a dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a list of gene symbols from my RNA-seq results: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, KRAS. For each symbol, fetch the official HGNC record and create a summary table with symbol, name, and HGNC ID.

output_examples.input: Fetch the gene record for TP53
output_examples.output: Returns a structured gene record including the official symbol TP53, full name 'tumor protein p53', gene family, locus type, and genomic coordinates from the HGNC database.

output_examples.input: Search HGNC for genes related to 'breast cancer'
output_examples.output: Returns a list of matching gene entries with their HGNC IDs, symbols, and relevance scores, including BRCA1, BRCA2, and related genes.

best_practices: Verify the gene symbol spelling before querying to avoid empty results from typo mismatches.
Use the specific field parameter when searching to narrow results to the most relevant matches.
Review the script output for error messages to confirm the API request was processed successfully.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-human gene lookups. HGNC only covers human genes and will return no results for other species.
Do not assume the script will work without network access. It requires connectivity to the HGNC REST API.
Do not skip input validation. Invalid or malformed terms may produce confusing error messages rather than clear failures.

faq.question: What is HGNC?
faq.answer: HGNC stands for HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee. It is the official authority for assigning standardized names and symbols to human genes.

faq.question: What data can I retrieve with this skill?
faq.answer: You can retrieve gene symbols, full names, HGNC IDs, gene family information, locus types, and genomic coordinates.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The HGNC REST API is publicly accessible without authentication.

faq.question: Can I search for multiple genes at the same time?
faq.answer: No. Each query handles one term at a time. For multiple genes, run separate queries in sequence.

faq.question: What happens if I search for a gene that does not exist?
faq.answer: The API returns an empty result set or an error message. The script reports this without raising an exception.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for non-human gene lookups?
faq.answer: No. HGNC only covers human genes. Use species-specific databases for other organisms.
